title: Paradigm raises $1.2 billion fourth fund, expanding beyond crypto into AI and robotics
blurb: Paradigm raised a $1.2B fourth fund that will invest in crypto alongside AI, robotics, and other frontier technology startups, its third venture fund and largest since the $2.5B vehicle it closed in 2021. The move follows Framework Ventures and Haun Ventures in broadening beyond digital assets, as crypto firms cite a maturing market and growing overlap with adjacent sectors.

title: INTERPOL-led global fraud crackdown arrests over 5,800, uncovers crypto laundering network tied to $122.5 million wallet
blurb: An INTERPOL-led operation across 97 countries arrested 5,811 people and intercepted $293 million in illicit assets, uncovering a crypto laundering network that used cross-chain swaps to obscure romance-scam proceeds. Thai police tied one 20-year-old suspect's wallet to more than $122.5 million processed in just 10 months, flagged through INTERPOL's I-GRIP mechanism for rapid virtual asset freezes.

title: EDX Markets Closes $76M Series C Led by SBI Holdings
blurb: EDX Markets, an institution-only crypto trading venue with its own central clearinghouse, closed a $76M Series C led by SBI Holdings, making the Tokyo-listed financial group a strategic investor. The capital funds expansion of EDX's trading, clearing, and settlement stack as it pursues a national trust bank charter via a pending OCC application for regulated custody.

title: Swift rolls out new blockchain ledger to bring 24/7 banking to 17 global giants
blurb: Swift opened its blockchain-based shared ledger for live testing with 17 banks including UBS, Citi, HSBC, BNP Paribas, and Wells Fargo, targeting round-the-clock cross-border payments using tokenized deposits. Final settlement still clears through existing rails, positioning the ledger as an always-on layer for regulated digital money rather than a replacement, and a bank-led counter to stablecoin transfers that already settle outside banking hours.

title: Cash App owner Block to pay $45 million after states say it made false security promises
blurb: Block agreed to pay $45 million to settle allegations from nearly all 50 states that it marketed Cash App as offering bank-grade security while failing to protect users from rampant fraud and lacking a functioning system to report scams. The settlement requires Block to provide 24-hour live customer support for fraud complaints and stop making safety claims about Cash App, though the company denies wrongdoing.

title: Lean Execution: a holistic approach to scale the world-computer
blurb: An ethresear.ch post details Lean Execution, one of three pillars of Justin Drake's Lean Ethereum proposal, which targets a minimal, SNARK-friendly, high-throughput execution layer built around zkEVMs, post-quantum cryptography, and formal verification. The piece frames execution scaling as a design problem in provable security and simplicity rather than raw block-size increases, arguing Ethereum can keep decentralization while raising throughput as developers reduce their own protocol influence.

title: Finding and Understanding Miscompilation Bugs in the Solidity Compiler
blurb: SolSmith, a semantics-aware differential fuzzer for the Solidity compiler, surfaced 25 miscompilation bugs over three years, some latent for multiple years. These are defects where the compiler emits incorrect bytecode from valid source, a distinct risk class for immutable contracts that pass source-level audits. The paper classifies each bug by root cause and end-user impact, with most tracing back to the optimizer's code-generation passes.

title: Crypto VC Paradigm launches $1.2 billion AI fund as it broadens beyond digital assets
blurb: Paradigm raised $1.2B for its third venture fund, targeting AI and robotics startups while continuing to back crypto after dedicated blockchain funds in 2021 and 2024. The fund has already deployed into drone-delivery firm Zipline, valued at $7.6B in January, and space-defense startup True Anomaly, which hit a $2.2B valuation in April.

title: Sony Bank gets conditional OCC approval for US trust bank to issue dollar-backed stablecoin
blurb: Sony Bank received conditional OCC approval to charter a US trust bank, Connectia Trust, positioning the Japanese lender to issue and manage a dollar-backed stablecoin pending final sign-off. The move adds a bank-regulated issuer to a US stablecoin market where GENIUS Act rules now favor chartered entities over offshore issuers.

title: Strike Launches Volatility-Proof Bitcoin Loans
blurb: Strike launched a bitcoin-backed lending product that removes price-triggered liquidations, keeping collateral untouched no matter how far BTC falls as long as borrowers stay current on payments. Missing an interest or maturity payment can trigger partial liquidation after a 10-day grace period, and the loans are available only as term loans in select US states, not lines of credit.

title: Hyundai Card completes its first real-world stablecoin pilot with Avalanche, Tether
blurb: Hyundai Card completed its first stablecoin remittance proof-of-concept, moving $20,000 in USDT on Avalanche between Hyundai Motor's US and Mexico entities in roughly seven minutes versus the three to four hours interbank transfers require. A second PoC across Hyundai's European entities begins later this month with Visa and Circle, testing multi-currency stablecoin settlement beyond the dollar.

title: Latin America’s biggest stock exchange now offers options on bitcoin, ether and solana futures
blurb: Brazil's B3, Latin America's largest stock exchange, launched options on bitcoin, ether, and solana futures on July 6, with bitcoin contracts denominated in reais and ether and solana in dollars. The options settle into the underlying futures rather than spot tokens, giving local traders and asset managers a regulated venue to hedge crypto exposure and trade volatility without routing to offshore markets.

title: European Commission looks to expand MiCA to cover emergence of tokenization, non-EU stablecoin issuers: report
blurb: The European Commission is seeking stakeholder comment until Sept. 30 on whether to expand MiCA to cover tokenized securities and non-EU stablecoin issuers, roughly a week after the framework took full effect on July 1. The review reflects developments since MiCA was drafted, including the US GENIUS Act's legalization of fully-backed payment stablecoins and the growing number of exchanges offering tokenized stocks.

title: NEAR Governance Votes to Scrap Developer Gas Rebate
blurb: NEAR's House of Stake governance passed HSP-027 to scrap the developer gas rebate, redirecting the 30% of smart-contract gas fees that previously went to contract owners into the burn, so 100% of gas fees are now destroyed. The change, expected around August 2026 with nearcore v2.14, makes NEAR issuance more deflationary and marks the on-chain body's first test of authority over the token's core economic parameters.

title: Kraken Wins $22M Arbitration Against Mazars
blurb: Kraken won a $22M arbitration award against Mazars, the accounting firm that abruptly dropped the exchange as a client in 2023 amid the banking-sector crackdown crypto insiders labeled Operation Choke Point 2.0. The ruling gives crypto firms a legal precedent for recovering damages when service providers exit on short notice, a recurring risk during periods of regulatory pressure on the industry.

title: Strike Launches Bitcoin Loans With No Price Liquidations
blurb: Strike launched "volatility-proof loans," a bitcoin-backed lending product that removes price-triggered liquidations for the life of the loan, keeping collateral untouched no matter how far bitcoin falls as long as the borrower keeps paying. The tradeoff is a lower 45% initial LTV, six-month terms, and roughly a 2.95-percentage-point rate premium over Strike's standard 7.49%-11.25% APR range.

title: Tether Invests $20M in Brazil's Mercado Bitcoin
blurb: Tether invested $20M in a strategic growth round for Mercado Bitcoin, Brazil's largest crypto exchange, to fund its expansion into tokenization, payments, credit, and on-chain capital markets across Latin America. The deal backs a platform serving 4.5 million users that has issued over R$2B ($370M) in tokenized assets and holds more than 10 regulatory licenses across Brazil and Europe.

title: How Reliable Is the Multi-Input Heuristic for Bitcoin Address Clustering in Law Enforcement Contexts?
blurb: This paper evaluates the multi-input heuristic, the standard technique for Bitcoin address clustering in blockchain forensics, against legally mandated ground-truth data from European crypto service providers. Restricted to reported addresses, the heuristic looks strong, recovering same-service pairs with 0.71 recall. But full-cluster metrics fall to 0.36 precision and 0.44 recall, with near-total failures for some entities, meaning MIH clusters used to justify seizures or trial evidence carry entity-dependent reliability.

title: BNB Chain builds new Layer 1 for agentic trading, targets 2027 mainnet
blurb: BNB Chain is building a new Layer 1 optimized for agentic trading, featuring sub-50ms preconfirmations and no public mempool to limit front-running by bots. The team is targeting testnet by the end of 2026 and mainnet in 2027, positioning the chain for autonomous agents executing high-frequency on-chain strategies.

title: Internal US DOJ memo: attorneys working on digital currency cases should expect less cooperation from Binance, which is adding requirements for asset freezes (Leo Schwartz/The Information)
blurb: An internal US DOJ memo told attorneys handling digital currency cases to expect reduced cooperation from Binance, which is adding new requirements before it will process asset freezes. The added friction complicates law enforcement efforts to seize funds tied to fraud and sanctions cases, potentially slowing seizures that previously relied on the exchange's voluntary compliance.

title: Gemini Launches 0% Stock Trading in Push to Become Super App
blurb: Gemini launched 0% commission stock trading for users in most US states, clearing through Apex Clearing as an FINRA-registered introducing broker, in a bid to become an "all-in-one financial super app." The move follows Coinbase and Kraken into equities and commodities, and lands weeks after Gemini won CFTC approval for DCM and DCO licenses to clear its own derivatives.

title: BitGo introduces quantum protection for institutional Bitcoin wallets
blurb: BitGo introduced quantum-protection tools for institutional Bitcoin wallets, expanding its multi-signature architecture with new methods to assess and reduce quantum-related exposure across UTXO-based wallets. The rollout, expected in the coming weeks, includes a patent-pending technique that groups and prioritizes UTXOs by address to limit exposure from partially spent funds, though it excludes Taproot and Pay-to-Public-Key addresses that reveal public keys onchain.

title: Sen. Wyden urges Senate leaders to preserve contested blockchain developer protections in broader crypto bill
blurb: Sen. Ron Wyden is pressing Senate leaders to keep a provision shielding blockchain software developers from money-transmitter liability in the broader crypto market-structure bill. The clause, contested by lawmakers worried about illicit-finance loopholes, would clarify that writing non-custodial protocol code does not trigger registration requirements applied to money-services businesses.

title: Fintech: Robinhood launches Eth chain for Stock tokens
blurb: Robinhood launched Robinhood Chain, an Ethereum-based network for trading tokenized stocks, and rolled out a set of on-chain trading primitives aimed at its 27M customers. The move pushes Robinhood into direct competition with Coinbase's Base for onboarding retail brokerage activity to on-chain rails, even as DeFi spot volumes sit down 71% year-over-year.

title: Binance Wallet adds Plume’s yield vault offering access to Invesco and Bitwise funds
blurb: Binance Wallet integrated Plume's yield vault, giving its users direct access to tokenized funds managed by Invesco and Bitwise. The addition routes on-chain deposits into regulated real-world asset strategies, extending Binance Wallet's DeFi surface beyond native crypto yield into institutional fixed-income and treasury products.

title: Cardano founding entity EMURGO steps down from Pentad governance role after wallet exploit
blurb: EMURGO, one of Cardano's three founding organizations, stepped down from the Pentad governance coalition to focus on recovering funds after an exploit drained roughly $2.4 million in ADA from 374 SecondFi wallets. The flaw hit the wallet's address generation system, and EMURGO says it expects to reimburse affected users over a two-week window.

title: SEC Adds Three Crypto Rules to 2026 Regulatory Agenda
blurb: The SEC added three crypto rulemakings to its 2026 Unified Regulatory Agenda, covering token offerings, broker-dealer capital rules, and market structure, each targeting a proposed-rule date of July 2026. The entries formalize the agency's stated priorities into specific RINs with a public timetable, though no rule text has been released and the July date is a target rather than a filing.

title: AscendEX crypto exchange shuts down, no assurance on user payouts
blurb: AscendEX ceased operations effective July 1, citing regulatory, financial, and operational challenges, and disclosed on July 6 that it cannot guarantee full repayment of user funds. The wind-down leaves customers without a clear recovery timeline and adds another failed venue to a year of mid-tier exchange closures.

title: Pump.fun Adds Trading for Robinhood Chain Tokens as CASHCAT Meme Coin Frenzy Builds
blurb: Pump.fun added support for trading Robinhood Chain tokens, extending the Solana launchpad's multichain tool to Robinhood's Arbitrum-based L2 with gas sponsored and no bridging required. The move follows a surge in CASHCAT, a meme coin referencing Robinhood's old mascot that climbed 700% to 950% on July 8, pushing its market cap from low millions to a $68M-$100M range.

title: Ethlabs is Online
blurb: Barnabé Monnot shared a week-two update on Ethlabs, the newly launched organization now in its fundraising phase.

title: ‘Best course of action:’ DeFi dashboard Zapper to shut down after 7 years
blurb: Zapper, the DeFi portfolio dashboard that raised a $15M Series A led by Framework Ventures in May 2021, is shutting down after seven years, with the team calling the closure the "best course of action." The wind-down marks another consolidation among first-generation DeFi analytics tools as free dashboards struggle to sustain revenue against integrated wallet and exchange front-ends.

title: India central bank seeks to bar financial institutions from exposure to crypto assets: Reuters
blurb: The Reserve Bank of India reasserted its call for a crypto policy "leaning towards prohibition," recommending that banks and financial institutions be barred from holding, trading, or gaining exposure to crypto assets and privately issued stablecoins, per Reuters. The stance persists despite India hosting nearly 39 million crypto investors holding roughly $2.1 billion in digital assets as of the end of May.

title: Paradigm Raises $1.2 Billion for Fourth Venture Fund
blurb: Paradigm raised $1.2B for its fourth fund, extending its mandate beyond crypto to invest across crypto, AI, and robotics from the earliest stages. The raise formalizes a shift for the firm behind the Foundry testing framework and Reth Ethereum client, and reflects a broader pattern of crypto-native VCs chasing compute and machine-learning infrastructure deals alongside their digital-asset bets.

title: BonkDAO Attacker Moves $19M Loot Into New 'BONK 2.0' DAO
blurb: Chainalysis traced BonkDAO's $20M governance attack to a wallet that borrowed and bought 1% of BONK's supply to pass a treasury-draining vote on July 6, then moved $19M of the loot into a new multisig it dubs "BONK 2.0." The multisig is controlled by the malicious voter, the exploiter wallet, and a third party tied to the voter, who has already liquidated $5.3M of the BONK acquired to secure voting power.

title: 'CASHCAT' trader turns $800 into over $1 million on Robinhood's brand new blockchain
blurb: A cat-themed memecoin called CASHCAT became the first breakout hit on Robinhood Chain, the brokerage's Arbitrum-based network that went live July 1, reaching a market value near $105 million while its five most profitable wallets realized roughly $3.7 million in gains. The token holds only about $6.6 million of Uniswap liquidity against that valuation, undercutting Robinhood's pitch to position the chain as infrastructure for tokenized real-world assets.

title: Singapore's Temasek investment fund says crypto is off the table, will focus on AI
blurb: Temasek, Singapore's $400B state investment fund, said it will prioritize AI over crypto, citing regulatory uncertainty and a $275M write-off from FTX's 2022 collapse, and plans to lift AI holdings from 6% to 15% of its portfolio by 2031. The fund holds no direct crypto positions today but said it will keep exploring blockchain applications.

title: BNB Chain is building a new layer-1 for high-frequency trading and AI agents
blurb: BNB Chain is building a new layer-1 blockchain for high-frequency trading and autonomous AI agents, targeting over 100,000 transactions per second, sub-50ms preconfirmations, and sub-second finality, with a testnet by end of 2026 and mainnet in early 2027. The design removes the public mempool and streams transactions directly to the block leader to cut latency and prevent front-running, aiming to give self-custodied traders execution speeds closer to centralized exchanges.

title: Judge rejects Kalshi attempt to override New York state gambling laws
blurb: A federal judge rejected Kalshi's request to block New York from enforcing its state gambling laws against the prediction market's sports-event contracts, ruling that Kalshi's CFTC registration as a designated contract market does not exempt it from state regulation. Kalshi is appealing to the 2nd Circuit, one of several cases testing whether federal oversight of prediction markets preempts state gambling law.

title: Reserve Bank of India still favors crypto prohibition to curtail tax evasion: Reuters
blurb: The Reserve Bank of India is still pushing a policy "leaning toward prohibition" on crypto, opposing bank exposure to digital assets and both dollar- and rupee-pegged stablecoins over contagion and seigniorage risks, according to government documents reviewed by Reuters. The stance persists despite nearly 39 million domestic investors holding roughly $2.1 billion in crypto as of May, with tax officials flagging that fewer than a quarter of crypto traders declared their gains.

title: AscendEX Halts Operations, Freezes Automated Withdrawals
blurb: AscendEX ceased operations on July 1 and moved all withdrawals to manual review from July 6, citing its lack of authorization under the EU's MiCA regime and a "strategic transaction" that was meant to supply liquidity but whose counterparty failed to perform. The exchange said it cannot guarantee the timing or amounts of withdrawals and flagged that a formal insolvency process, which would govern customer balances, could follow.

title: Robinhood Chain Metrics Surge as the Network Leans Into Memecoins
blurb: Robinhood Chain's onchain activity spiked this week, with cumulative addresses climbing toward 200,000 and TVL reaching roughly $234M just over a week after its July 1 public launch on Arbitrum. The surge followed a Pump.fun integration, a memecoin frenzy, and CEO Vlad Tenev's reversal to endorse memes on a network he had marketed for tokenized real-world assets.

title: Dinari, tZERO join forces on turnkey platform for tokenized U.S. equities
blurb: Dinari and tZERO partnered to offer broker-dealers a turnkey platform for tokenized U.S. equities, bundling issuance, trading, custody, clearing, and settlement into one regulated framework. Dinari's dShares are backed one-for-one by shares held with regulated custodians and preserve dividends and corporate actions, positioning the pair between offshore synthetic-token models like xStocks and issuer-sponsored tokenization pushed by Securitize.

title: Vitalik Proposes the Extremely Lean Consensus Chain
blurb: Vitalik Buterin proposed the "Extremely Lean" consensus chain, a design that pushes validator state management to stakers who occasionally ZK-prove their own state, eliminating expensive end-of-epoch beacon chain processing. Building on Lean upgrades like single-slot finality and STARK-based signature aggregation, the approach elides validator pubkeys from state and could scale Ethereum consensus to millions of validators.

title: Traders Sue Polymarket, CEO Coplan Over Strategy Market Decision
blurb: Two Polymarket users sued the platform, its holding entities Adventure One QSS and Blockratize, and CEO Shayne Coplan in New York state court, alleging the site wrongly resolved a market on whether Strategy sold Bitcoin. The plaintiffs held "Yes" shares and point to a Strategy 8-K disclosing a 32 BTC sale, seeking the denied $1-per-share redemption plus treble damages under New York consumer-protection law.

title: Privy Launches Global Fiat Onramps
blurb: Privy launched global fiat onramps, giving each user a single persistent deposit address that accepts crypto across supported chains and automatically bridges and swaps incoming funds into the token an app expects, with no KYC on the deposit path. The feature collapses the usual bridge-switch-swap onboarding into one deposit step, extending Privy's programmable-wallet stack that already provisions over 1 million agent accounts for platforms like ATXP.

title: Crypto VC firm Paradigm raises $1.2B to invest in ‘technical frontier’ startups
blurb: Paradigm raised $1.2B for its third venture fund, expanding beyond crypto to invest in AI and robotics alongside blockchain and agent tooling. The fund came in below the $1.5B target reported in February, and has already backed drone-delivery firm Zipline and space startup True Anomaly.

title: Strategy’s 3,588 BTC sale puts future bitcoin selling in focus: analysts
blurb: Strategy sold 3,588 BTC last week, its first bitcoin sale in months, shifting analyst attention to whether the company's treasury holdings could become a source of forced selling. CF Benchmarks noted that the sales are manageable while discretionary, but would raise concern if they stop being a choice and become necessary to service debt or preferred dividends.

title: Swift launches blockchain ledger for tokenized deposit pilot with 17 banks
blurb: Swift launched a blockchain-based shared ledger with 17 banks including Citi, HSBC, UBS, and Wells Fargo, its first live pilot for cross-border payments using bank-issued tokenized deposits. The ledger acts as an orchestration layer that moves tokenized deposits 24/7 across banks' own ledgers before final settlement runs through existing rails, preserving current compliance, credit, and risk controls.

title: Swift rolls out new blockchain ledger to bring 24/7 banking to 17 global giants
blurb: Swift opened its blockchain-based shared ledger to live testing with 17 banks including HSBC, UBS, Citi, and Wells Fargo, enabling round-the-clock cross-border payments using tokenized deposits. Funds can move overnight and on weekends while final settlement still runs through existing rails, positioning bank-issued tokenized deposits as a regulated alternative to stablecoins for always-on transfers.

title: Cash App owner Block to pay $45 million after states say it made false security promises
blurb: Block agreed to pay $45M to settle claims from regulators in nearly all 50 states, led by the New York Attorney General, that it misled Cash App users into believing their funds carried bank-grade protections while failing to build adequate fraud detection or customer support. The settlement requires Block to maintain 24/7 live fraud support and stop advertising Cash App's purported safety, a warning to neobanks about how they frame deposit and fraud protections.

title: AT&T, T-Mobile US, and Verizon ditch SMS for network-based authentication
blurb: AT&T, T-Mobile US, and Verizon, through the Ericsson-backed API venture Aduna, launched a network-based number-verification service that authenticates users at the carrier level to replace SMS one-time passcodes. Aduna offers a single API across all three carriers to cut OTP wait times and targets the $15.9B in fraud losses U.S. consumers reported to the FTC last year, positioning telcos as authentication infrastructure for fintechs.

title: INTERPOL-led global fraud crackdown arrests over 5,800, uncovers crypto laundering network tied to $122.5 million wallet
blurb: INTERPOL's Operation First Light, spanning 97 countries from January to April, arrested 5,811 people and intercepted $293 million in illicit assets tied to social engineering scams and the money laundering that moves their proceeds. Investigators traced one Thai suspect's wallet that processed over $122.5 million in 10 months through cross-chain token swaps, underscoring how romance-scam laundering now routes through crypto rails that demand chain-analytics tooling to unwind.

title: Strike Launches Volatility-Proof Bitcoin Loans
blurb: Strike launched a bitcoin-backed lending product that eliminates price-triggered liquidations, letting borrowers keep their BTC collateral untouched no matter how far the price falls as long as they stay current on payments. Collateral can still be partially liquidated if a borrower misses an interest or maturity payment and fails to cure within a 10-day grace period, and the loans are available only as term loans in select US states.

title: EDX Markets Closes $76M Series C Led by SBI Holdings
blurb: EDX Markets closed a $76M Series C led by SBI Holdings, making the Tokyo-listed group a strategic investor in the institutional-only crypto venue and its central clearinghouse. The round follows EDX's OCC application for EDX Trust, a proposed national trust bank for regulated custody, clearing, and settlement, positioning the exchange to court institutions as SBI expands its yen-stablecoin and RLUSD ecosystem.

title: Hyundai Card completes its first real-world stablecoin pilot with Avalanche, Tether
blurb: Hyundai Card completed its first stablecoin remittance proof-of-concept, converting $20,000 into USDT on Avalanche to move funds from Hyundai Motor America to its Mexico affiliate in roughly seven minutes versus the three to four hours interbank transfers require. A second PoC starts later this month across Hyundai's European entities with Visa and Circle, testing non-dollar stablecoin settlement and its cost efficiency against traditional rails.

title: Latin America’s biggest stock exchange now offers options on bitcoin, ether and solana futures
blurb: Brazil's B3, Latin America's largest stock exchange, launched options on bitcoin, ether, and solana futures on July 6, adding to its growing suite of regulated crypto derivatives. The contracts settle into the underlying futures rather than spot tokens, involving no custody or transfer, which gives local traders and asset managers a domestic venue to hedge crypto exposure and trade volatility without routing through offshore markets.

title: Sony Bank gets conditional OCC approval for US trust bank to issue dollar-backed stablecoin
blurb: The OCC granted Sony Bank conditional approval to establish Connectia Trust, a US national trust bank chartered to issue and manage a dollar-backed stablecoin, pending final signoff. The move gives a foreign banking giant a regulated federal path into US stablecoin issuance, intensifying competition with domestic issuers and fintechs racing to secure charters under the new regime.

title: European Commission looks to expand MiCA to cover emergence of tokenization, non-EU stablecoin issuers: report
blurb: The European Commission is seeking stakeholder comment until Sept. 30 on whether to expand MiCA to cover tokenized securities and non-EU stablecoin issuers, roughly a week after the crypto framework took full effect. The revision reflects developments since the rules were drafted, including the US GENIUS Act's payments-stablecoin regime, and signals the EU wants MiCA to govern tokenized stocks and foreign issuers already courting the bloc.

title: BNB Chain builds new Layer 1 for agentic trading, targets 2027 mainnet
blurb: BNB Chain is building a new Layer 1 purpose-built for agentic trading, with sub-50ms preconfirmations and no public mempool to blunt front-running, targeting testnet by end-2026 and mainnet in 2027. The design signals a bet that autonomous trading agents need dedicated low-latency infrastructure rather than general-purpose chains, positioning BNB Chain against latency-focused rivals like Solana and MegaETH.

title: Block agrees to pay $45M and offer live customer support for Cash App to settle claims by 46 US states that the company failed to protect users from fraud (Daniel Wiessner/Reuters)
blurb: Block agreed to pay $45M and add live customer support to Cash App to settle claims from 46 US states that it failed to protect users from fraud and process disputes properly. The multistate settlement signals mounting regulatory pressure on neobanks and P2P apps to staff up fraud-resolution and dispute-handling functions that traditional banks have long been required to maintain.

title: Kraken Wins $22M Arbitration Against Mazars
blurb: Kraken won a $22M arbitration award against Mazars after the accounting firm abruptly dropped the exchange as a client during the 2023 banking crackdown known as Operation Choke Point 2.0. The ruling gives crypto firms a legal precedent to pursue damages when service providers exit under regulatory pressure, raising the cost of debanking-style moves against digital-asset businesses.

title: Fintech: Robinhood launches Eth chain for Stock tokens
blurb: Robinhood launched Robinhood Chain, an Ethereum-based network for issuing tokenized stocks, extending on-chain trading primitives to its 27M customers and pushing the broker directly into DeFi infrastructure. The move puts Robinhood in competition with Coinbase's Base for tokenized-asset rails, and lands as decentralized-exchange spot volumes sit down 71% YoY, betting on-chain equities can revive trading activity.

title: Strike Launches Bitcoin Loans With No Price Liquidations
blurb: Strike launched "volatility-proof loans," a bitcoin-backed lending product that removes price-triggered liquidations, so collateral stays untouched however far bitcoin falls as long as the borrower keeps paying. The protection costs borrowers a lower 45% initial LTV, six-month terms, and roughly a 2.95-point rate premium over Strike's standard 7.49% to 11.25% APR, with missed payments still triggering liquidation after a 10-day grace period.

title: Internal US DOJ memo: attorneys working on digital currency cases should expect less cooperation from Binance, which is adding requirements for asset freezes (Leo Schwartz/The Information)
blurb: A leaked internal DOJ memo instructs prosecutors on digital-currency cases to expect reduced cooperation from Binance, which is now imposing additional requirements before it will execute asset freezes. The friction marks a shift for an exchange that pledged deeper compliance after its 2023 settlement, and it complicates enforcement workflows that have leaned on rapid exchange-level freezes to recover illicit funds.

title: Tether Invests $20M in Brazil's Mercado Bitcoin
blurb: Tether invested $20M in a strategic growth round for Mercado Bitcoin, Brazil's largest crypto exchange, to fund its push into tokenization, payments, credit, and on-chain capital markets across Latin America. The deal extends Tether's pattern of taking stakes in regional financial infrastructure, following its $200M investment in Whop, and backs a platform that holds over 10 licenses including a Banco Central do Brasil Payment Institution license.

title: BitGo introduces quantum protection for institutional Bitcoin wallets
blurb: BitGo introduced a suite of quantum-protection tools for institutional Bitcoin wallets, extending its multi-signature architecture with new methods for assessing and reducing quantum-related exposure across UTXO-based wallets. The centerpiece, covered by a provisional patent, groups and prioritizes UTXOs by address to limit exposure from partially spent funds, though it excludes Taproot and Pay-to-Public-Key addresses that reveal a public key onchain from the start.

title: Gemini Launches 0% Stock Trading in Push to Become Super App
blurb: Gemini launched 0% commission stock trading for users in most U.S. states, registering with FINRA as an introducing broker and clearing trades through Apex Clearing, as it pushes to become an all-in-one financial super app. The move follows rivals Coinbase and Kraken into equities and mirrors the industry-standard fee model pioneered by Robinhood, which typically monetizes through payment for order flow, margin lending, and interest on idle cash.

title: Sen. Wyden urges Senate leaders to preserve contested blockchain developer protections in broader crypto bill
blurb: Sen. Ron Wyden is pressing Senate leaders to keep a provision shielding blockchain software developers from money-transmitter liability inside the broader crypto market-structure bill. The fight signals that developer-liability protections remain a live sticking point, and their fate will shape whether builders of non-custodial protocols face the same KYC/AML obligations as regulated financial intermediaries.

title: Binance Wallet adds Plume’s yield vault offering access to Invesco and Bitwise funds
blurb: Binance Wallet integrated Plume's yield vault, giving users direct access to tokenized real-world-asset funds managed by Invesco and Bitwise. The move pushes regulated fund exposure into a mainstream crypto wallet, letting Binance users earn on-chain yield from traditional asset managers without leaving the app or routing through a separate brokerage.

title: AscendEX crypto exchange shuts down, no assurance on user payouts
blurb: AscendEX ceased all platform operations effective July 1, citing regulatory, financial, and operational challenges, and has given users no assurance that account balances will be returned. The shutdown leaves customers without a clear claims process or timeline for payouts, a reminder that centralized exchange balances carry counterparty risk that neobank or bank-held funds do not.

title: India central bank seeks to bar financial institutions from exposure to crypto assets: Reuters
blurb: The Reserve Bank of India reasserted its call for a crypto policy "leaning towards prohibition," seeking to bar banks and financial institutions from holding, trading, or gaining exposure to crypto assets and privately issued stablecoins, according to Reuters. The RBI also warned that foreign-currency-backed stablecoins threaten monetary sovereignty, a hard line that keeps India's roughly 39 million crypto investors and $2.1B in holdings outside the regulated financial system.

title: SEC Adds Three Crypto Rules to 2026 Regulatory Agenda
blurb: The SEC listed three crypto-focused rulemakings in its 2026 Unified Regulatory Agenda, covering crypto asset offerings, broker-dealer capital and customer-protection rules, and market-structure amendments, each targeting a proposed rule as soon as July. The agenda formalizes priorities from the SEC's June draft strategic plan into specific RIN numbers and a public timetable, though no rule text exists yet and the July date is a target rather than a filing.

title: ‘Best course of action:’ DeFi dashboard Zapper to shut down after 7 years
blurb: Zapper, the DeFi portfolio dashboard that raised a $15M Series A led by Framework Ventures in May 2021, is shutting down after seven years. The team called winding down the "best course of action," marking another consolidation among crypto analytics tools as free on-chain data and wallet-native tracking eroded the standalone dashboard model.

title: BNB Chain is building a new layer-1 for high-frequency trading and AI agents
blurb: BNB Chain is building a new layer-1 blockchain optimized for high-frequency trading and autonomous AI agents, targeting over 100,000 transactions per second, sub-second finality, and preconfirmations under 50 milliseconds, with a public testnet by end of 2026 and mainnet in early 2027. The design removes the public mempool and streams transactions directly to the block leader, cutting latency and blocking front-running to give self-custodied traders execution speeds closer to centralized exchanges.

title: Wall Street Banks Miss Multibillion-Dollar AI Deals in Hong Kong
blurb: Chinese AI companies raised $5.8B in Hong Kong this week, with Wall Street banks largely absent from the underwriting and advisory roles on the deals. The gap reflects how US sanctions and geopolitical restrictions have ceded Chinese AI financing to regional and local banks, cutting American institutions out of one of the fastest-growing capital-raising markets.

title: Judge rejects Kalshi attempt to override New York state gambling laws
blurb: A federal judge in the Southern District of New York rejected Kalshi's bid to block enforcement of state gambling laws against its sports-event prediction contracts, ruling that CFTC registration as a designated contract market does not preempt state regulation. Kalshi is appealing to the 2nd Circuit, one of several cases testing whether federal oversight shields prediction markets from state gaming rules and shaping how far the CFTC-regulated model can expand.

title: 'CASHCAT' trader turns $800 into over $1 million on Robinhood's brand new blockchain
blurb: Robinhood launched its Arbitrum-based Robinhood Chain on July 1 to move stocks and bonds onchain, but the network's first breakout is CASHCAT, a memecoin named after the mascot the company used before rebranding, now at roughly a $105M market value. Early wallets turned tiny stakes into seven-figure gains, with the top five banking about $3.7M against just $6.6M of Uniswap liquidity, undercutting Robinhood's real-world-asset pitch.

title: Reserve Bank of India still favors crypto prohibition to curtail tax evasion: Reuters
blurb: The Reserve Bank of India is still pushing for a policy "leaning toward prohibition" on crypto, opposing bank exposure to digital assets and both dollar- and rupee-pegged stablecoins over contagion, seigniorage, and capital-outflow risks, according to government documents reviewed by Reuters. The stance holds despite nearly 39 million domestic investors holding roughly $2.1B in crypto, with tax officials citing widespread underreporting and the difficulty of tracing offshore and peer-to-peer transactions.

title: OFAC Sanctions 134 ISIS-K Crypto Wallet Addresses as Tether Freezes Funds
blurb: OFAC added 134 crypto wallet addresses tied to ISIS-K to its SDN list, including 131 Tron addresses that took in over $1.4M in donations since 2023, prompting Tether to freeze the associated balances. The action shows how quickly stablecoin issuers now move to enforce sanctions on-chain, giving OFAC a faster freeze mechanism than traditional rails while raising Tether's role as a de facto compliance chokepoint.

title: Lenders Seek $700 Million After Japanese Firm’s Collapse
blurb: Creditors across Japan are seeking to recover more than $700 million from a payments firm that collapsed into bankruptcy this week, after it allegedly falsified financial statements for at least 20 years. The failure exposes how weak audit oversight can let a settlement intermediary mask insolvency for decades, leaving lenders and merchants scrambling to reconstruct where customer funds actually went.

title: Dinari, tZERO join forces on turnkey platform for tokenized U.S. equities
blurb: Dinari and tZERO partnered to offer broker-dealers a turnkey platform for tokenized U.S. equities, combining Dinari's dShares issuance with tZERO's brokerage, custody, clearing, and settlement infrastructure. The bundle lets financial firms launch blockchain-based equity offerings without assembling the underlying market plumbing, positioning Dinari's one-for-one custodied model against the synthetic-token and issuer-sponsored approaches pursued by Robinhood, Kraken, and Securitize.

title: Revolut hires ex-Chase UK boss Kuba Fast to lead its European bank
blurb: Revolut appointed Kuba Fast, the former CEO of JPMorgan's Chase UK, to run Revolut Bank UAB, the Lithuania-based entity holding its European banking licence and supervised by the ECB and Bank of Lithuania. Fast will also serve as CEO for Europe, replacing Joe Heneghan, with the move expected mid-2026 pending ECB and Bank of Lithuania sign-off, as Revolut builds out its stack after securing a UK banking licence earlier in 2026.

title: Europe’s biggest new funds in H1 2026
blurb: Europe's largest new venture funds in H1 2026 concentrated on scaleups, AI, and climate, with managers closing fewer but larger vehicles as LPs stayed cautious amid slower exits and thinner distributions. The shift signals a flight to established managers and later-stage bets, squeezing first-time funds and early-stage GPs who now face a tighter, more selective capital environment across the region.
